steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim states that My Name Is Red juxtaposes literary realism with Turkish folklore. The correct quotation must show both elements actively present together — not just that one influenced the other, but that Turkish folklore intrudes into an otherwise realistic plot.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"Although Turkish folklore clearly informs the style and occasionally antirealistic plot of *My Name Is Red*, the novel also shows the inarguable influence of the magical realist tradition of Latin America."’: credits both Turkish folklore and Latin American magical realism as influences, but doesn’t show the two elements (realism + folklore) in direct tension within the novel itself. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"The logic of the realistic plot of *My Name Is Red* is repeatedly and productively disrupted by the presence of imagery and situations drawn from Turkish folklore."’: directly demonstrates juxtaposition — a realistic plot disrupted by Turkish folklore. This matches the claim precisely. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘"Like many works in the Latin American magical realist tradition, *My Name Is Red* is indebted to antirealistic elements in the folkloric tradition of Turkey."’: frames folklore as a debt or influence rather than showing it actively disrupting the realistic mode; doesn’t capture the live tension of juxtaposition. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"While *My Name Is Red* alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes of representation, details suggesting the influence of Turkish folklore nevertheless occur throughout the novel."’: mentions alternation rather than juxtaposition, and the “nevertheless” framing weakens the connection between folklore and the realistic plot. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
